channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Number of dead and missing still unclear as first aid arrives in quake-hit Pacific nation of Vanuatu
item.description: The number of people killed and missing remains unclear two days after a magnitude 7.3 earthquake struck the Pacific island nation of Vanuatu.
item.dc:creator: Charlotte Graham-Mclay, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Japanese newspaper boss who influenced the nation's postwar politics died at 98
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Tsuneo Watanabe, the powerful head of Japan's largest newspaper who had close ties with the country's powerful conservative leaders, has died, his company said Thursday. He was 98.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Separated for decades, Assad's fall spurs hope for families split by Golan Heights buffer zone
item.description: When Israel seized the strategic Golan Heights from Syria in 1967, many families were separated by a buffer zone created between the Israeli-controlled region and the rest of Syria.
item.dc:creator: Elena Becatoros, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Israeli airstrikes hit Yemen's rebel-held capital and port city after Houthi attack targets Israel
item.description: At least nine people have been killed in Israeli attacks on Yemen, a Houthi-controlled satellite channel reported Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Researcher: North Korea can produce ballistic missiles for Russia to use against Ukraine in months
item.description: North Korea demonstrated this year that it could produce ballistic missiles and supply them to Russia for use against Ukraine in a matter of months.
item.dc:creator: Edith M. Lederer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: British PM Starmer urges Trump to stand with Ukraine
item.description: The United Kingdom’s Prime Minister Keir Starmer is urging President-elect Trump to stand with Ukraine in its war against Russia. Starmer and Trump spoke on the phone Wednesday, their second call since Trump won the presidential election last month.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: French high court upholds ex-president's corruption conviction
item.description: A Court of Cassation decision upheld the corruption conviction of former French President Nicolas Sarkozy, who now faces a year in prison.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cyclone Chido leaves Mayotte reeling. Warmer oceans fueled it
item.description: The Indian Ocean archipelago of Mayotte is reeling from Cyclone Chido, the most intense storm to hit the French territory in 90 years.
item.dc:creator: TAIWO ADEBAYO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel orders another Gaza evacuation ahead of an offensive
item.description: The Israeli military on Wednesday ordered Palestinians to evacuate another section of central Gaza ahead of an offensive in the built-up Bureij refugee camp, even as Israel and the Hamas appeared to inch closer to a ceasefire in the 14-month war.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Kenyan prisoner released from Guantánamo after 18 years without charges
item.description: A Kenyan prisoner was released from the detention facility at Guantánamo Bay on Tuesday after spending more than a decade imprisoned without charges. Mohammed Abdul Malik Bajabu, 51, served 17 years at Guantánamo following accusations of involvement with al Qaeda’s attack on tourists in East Africa.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: New charges target ex-Miami congressman for lobbying on behalf of sanctioned Venezuelan tycoon
item.description: A grand jury in Washington has returned an indictment against former Congressman David Rivera for failing to register as a foreign agent of a sanctioned Venezuelan media tycoon on whose behalf the Miami Republican allegedly lobbied the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Goodman, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: What Assad's fall has revealed about Syria's trade in the stimulant drug Captagon
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Since the fall of former Syrian President Bashar Assad, industrial-scale manufacturing facilities of the amphetamine-like stimulant Captagon have been uncovered around the country, which experts say fed a $10 billion annual global trade in the highly addictive drug.
item.dc:creator: SALLY ABOU ALJOUD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia says it has detained a suspect in the Moscow bombing that killed a senior general
item.description: Russia's security service said Wednesday it detained a citizen from Uzbekistan in the bombing that killed a senior general as he left his southeastern Moscow apartment — a bold assassination that was claimed by Ukraine's security service. Lt. Gen.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Russia's Syria exit could help Ukraine-Israel relationship as analyst warns it 'offers little' to Jerusalem
item.description: With Russia’s diminished influence in Syria, Ukraine calls on Israel to reconsider its stance and provide essential defensive weapons and technology.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Animal welfare group says bacterial infection killed Sonia the elephant in Pakistan
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — Pakistani veterinary surgeons found that a bacterial infection killed an elephant at a safari park in the city of Karachi earlier this month, a veterinarian with a global animal welfare organization said Wednesday. Dr.
item.dc:creator: MUNIR AHMED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Japanese space startup aborts 2nd satellite launch attempt minutes after liftoff
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — A Japanese space startup said its second attempt to launch a rocket carrying satellites into orbit had been aborted minutes after liftoff Wednesday and destroyed itself again, nine months after the company's first launch attempt ended in an explosion. Space One's Kairos No.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Filipina who won a last-minute reprieve from an Indonesian firing squad returns home
item.description: JAKARTA, Indonesia (AP) — A Filipina woman who had been on death row in Indonesia, and who was nearly executed by firing squad in 2015, arrived home early Wednesday after longstanding requests from her home country.
item.dc:creator: NINIEK KARMINI and TERESA CEROJANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Canada's prime minister faces calls to resign. Here's what could happen next
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau faces growing pressure to resign from his own Liberal Party after his top minister abruptly quit and criticized his handling of the budget.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rescuers dig for survivors of Vanuatu earthquake with no water and unclear death toll
item.description: Vanuatu’s capital is without water after reservoirs were destroyed by a violent magnitude 7.3 earthquake that wrought havoc on the South Pacific island nation.
item.dc:creator: Charlotte Graham-Mclay, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: 2 charged in deadly drone attack on US soldiers in Jordan
item.description: The U.S. on Monday charged two Iranian officials in a scheme that contributed to a deadly January drone attack that killed three American service members in Jordan. The Department of Justice (DOJ) charged Mahdi Mohammad Sadeghi, 42, who is a U.S.-Iranian national from Natick, Mass.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Netanyahu spokesperson denies Cairo trip amid Gaza ceasefire push
item.description: A spokesperson for Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Tuesday rejected reports the Israeli leader was traveling to Cairo for negotiations over a ceasefire and hostage release deal with Hamas in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: DHS overhauls H-1B visa program
item.description: The Biden administration announced a rule Tuesday clarifying who can apply for an H-1B work visa, a key program to attract international talent that’s been criticized as too complicated and susceptible to abuse.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, The Hill

item.title: US Embassy seriously damaged in Vanuatu earthquake
item.description: A forceful 7.4 magnitude earthquake shook the capital of Vanuatu damaging the newly established U.S. Embassy on Tuesday. Authorities reported the earthquake and subsequent aftershocks at 12:53 p.m. local time, according to an embassy alert.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Ukrainian official takes credit after Russian general Igor Kirillov killed by explosive device in Moscow
item.description: An official with the Ukrainian Security Services says the agency was behind the bombing assassination of Lt. Gen. Igor Kirillov, commander of Russia’s chemical, biological and radiation defense forces, and his assistant in Moscow on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Canadian Finance Minister resigns as Trudeau government's popularity flounders
item.description: Canadian Finance Minister Chrystia Freeland announced her resignation from Justin Trudeau's Cabinet on Monday, a powerful blow to the Liberal Prime Minister's hold on the government.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump says Turkey ‘did an unfriendly takeover’ in Syria as US-brokered cease-fire appears to fail
item.description: President-elect Trump described the fall of the Assad regime in Syria as an "unfriendly takeover" by neighboring Turkey as sources tell Fox News Digital the U.S.-brokered cease-fire has collapsed.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Assad paints himself as 'custodian' to Syria as picture unfolds on collapse of Damascus
item.description: Former Syrian President Bashar al-Assad broke his silence since fleeing the nation following the Islamist rebel takeover and looked to portray himself only ever as a "custodian" of Syria.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: FIFA apologizes to Ukraine soccer body for Crimea map error at 2026 World Cup draw
item.description: “While the incident was unintentional, we sincerely regret any concern it may have cause,” FIFA’s chief member association officer wrote in a letter .
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Russian paramilitary soldiers killed in friendly fire attack by North Koreans after enlisting DPRK help
item.description: North Korean troops recently killed Russian soldiers in a friendly fire attack, according to Ukrainian intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mother of an American journalist imprisoned in Syria sees hope following news of Travis Timmerman's release
item.description: The mother of Austin Tice, an American journalist captured in Syria, said the news of Travis Timmerman's recent prison release has sparked new hope that her son will soon walk free.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Reporter's Notebook: Chronicling the Assad regime from death of the father to defeat of the son
item.description: Fox News senior foreign affairs correspondent Greg Palkot documents his years of reporting from Syria in the aftermath of the fall of the regime of Bashar Assad.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Capitol rioter who tried to join Russian army is sentenced to prison for probation violation
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A Dallas man who tried to fly overseas to join the Russian military and fight against Ukraine was sentenced on Friday to six months in prison for violating the terms of his probation for storming the U.S. Capitol four years ago. Kevin Loftus, a 56-year-old veteran of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: American who says he crossed into Syria by foot is free after 7 months
item.description: An American who turned up in Syria on Thursday says he was detained after crossing into the country by foot on a Christian pilgrimage seven months ago.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9
